definitions.gloss_en: a movement action in which a unit moves forward toward the enemy
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}が{敵|てき}{方|がた}向に{移|い}{動|どう}する{行|こう}{動|どう}
definitions.explanation_en: A standard movement action. Distinct from 行軍 (march), which implies a longer-distance strategic move. In some rulesets, 前進 is a specific action with a defined move distance.
definitions.implicit_notes: 前進 is a classical Japanese military command meaning 'forward march' or 'advance'. It carries the connotation of deliberate, goal-directed movement toward the enemy rather than repositioning.
definitions.orthography_notes: 前進: 前 (forward/front) + 進 (advance/proceed). Goo dictionary military definition. JWC wargame movement documentation attests 前進 as a standard movement term. WH Japan uses 全力移動 for the Advance action — 前進 is therefore independent.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In miniature wargames, 前進 is typically a shorter advance move (as opposed to 行軍, which allows more distance but restricts other actions). Occurs in the 移動段階.

usage_jp: {前|ぜん}{進|しん}する (to advance); {前|ぜん}{進|しん}を{宣|せん}{言|げん}する (declare an advance)
comparative_notes: 行軍 (kougun, march) implies longer strategic movement at the cost of other actions. 前進 is the shorter tactical advance. WH Japan uses 全力移動 for a comparable action, so 前進 is an independent term.

examples.japanese: {彼|かれ}らは{川|かわ}まで{前|ぜん}{進|しん}した。

cross_references.note: 行軍 — march (longer movement, contrasted with 前進)
移動段階 — the phase in which 前進 occurs
